skill.name: skill-auditor
skill.description: Audits skill definitions for structure, quality, completeness, and maintainability. Use it to validate SKILL.md files, score skill readiness, and decide when skills should be split.
skill.summary: Review AI skill definitions for quality, structure, and readiness before distribution.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: The static high-risk weak-cryptography alerts are false positives caused by metadata and documentation words. The skill is publishable, but it should carry a warning because it documents shell commands and includes a local filesystem audit script.

medium_findings.title: Shell Remediation Examples Can Modify Files
medium_findings.description: The reference guide includes rm -rf and chmod examples for cleanup and script permissions. They are documented fixes, not hidden execution, but they can delete or modify files if used without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit in documentation and can affect the filesystem. The risk is operational because no code path executes them automatically.

low_findings.title: Documented Command Examples Are Not Hidden Execution
low_findings.description: Static external-command findings in SKILL.md are fenced command examples for listing skills and running the auditor. The Python script itself does not invoke a shell or subprocess.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is in markdown examples, and scripts/audit_skills.py contains no subprocess, os.system, or shell execution calls.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The high-risk weak-cryptography alerts match words such as description and markdown context. No hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or credential handling code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe skill metadata fields or documentation text. There is no cryptographic implementation at those locations.

low_findings.title: Local Filesystem Inspection Is Core Functionality
low_findings.description: The bundled auditor resolves a user-supplied path, lists skill directories, reads SKILL.md, and checks resource folders. This is expected for an audit tool but should run only on trusted local paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem behavior is directly visible and necessary for auditing skills. No evidence shows network transfer or secret harvesting.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alert Has No Supporting Behavior
low_findings.description: The system-reconnaissance alert points to an error message for invalid YAML frontmatter. No host fingerprinting, environment dumping, or reconnaissance command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line only appends a validation error string. Nearby code parses frontmatter and does not inspect host identity or system configuration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Remediation Examples Can Modify Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The reference guide includes rm -rf and chmod examples for cleanup and script permissions. They are documented fixes, not hidden execution, but they can delete or modify files if used without review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit in documentation and can affect the filesystem. The risk is operational because no code path executes them automatically.

content.value_statement: Skill definitions can drift into unclear instructions, missing metadata, or oversized workflows. This skill reviews structure, scores readiness, and recommends focused improvements.
actual_capabilities: Checks SKILL.md frontmatter for required name and description fields.
Scores skill quality using documented deductions and readiness bands.
Detects TODO placeholders, short descriptions, and missing workflow sections.
Reviews bundled resources for empty directories and script permissions.
Flags skills that may need decomposition into smaller focused skills.
Generates human-readable or JSON audit summaries from the bundled script.
limitations: It focuses on skill quality and structure, not full security testing.
It needs local filesystem access to the skill directories under review.
The automated script does not replace manual judgment about usefulness.
The documented --fix workflow is not implemented in the bundled parser.

use_cases.title: Pre-Publish Skill Review
use_cases.description: Review a submitted skill for metadata, structure, documentation quality, and readiness before publishing.

use_cases.title: Repository-Wide Skill Audit
use_cases.description: Run a consistent audit across many local skills and compare scores, warnings, and decomposition signals.

use_cases.title: Skill Refactoring Guidance
use_cases.description: Identify bloated or mixed-purpose skills and plan focused sub-skills with clearer responsibilities.

prompt_templates.title: Check One Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single skill folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this skill folder for required SKILL.md metadata, structure, and obvious quality issues. Summarize the score and fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Review A Skill Collection
prompt_templates.scenario: Batch audit for a local skills directory
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the skill auditor on this skills directory. Report failing skills, common warnings, and the highest priority fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Decomposition
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate whether this skill should be split. Use word count, workflow complexity, concern count, and reusable resources as evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Marketplace Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a marketplace-ready audit summary with quality score, blocking issues, warnings, decomposition advice, and author-facing remediation steps.

output_examples.input: A skill has frontmatter, a clear workflow, and one short description warning.
output_examples.output: Quality score: 9 out of 10. Publishable with one suggested improvement: expand the description with clearer trigger conditions.

output_examples.input: A skill contains many unrelated workflows and exceeds the recommended word count.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: split the skill. Create focused sub-skills for separate workflows and keep shared guidance in references.

output_examples.input: A repository contains several skills with missing sections and remaining TODO markers.
output: Fix missing When to Use sections first.
Replace TODO placeholders with concrete instructions.

best_practices: Run the automated audit before manual review so structural issues are visible early.
Review command examples before execution, especially cleanup or permission changes.
Use the decomposition guidance when a skill mixes several unrelated workflows.
anti_patterns: Treating the numeric score as the only publication decision.
Running cleanup commands on broad paths without checking the target directory.
Using the auditor as a substitute for security, privacy, or license review.

faq.question: Does this skill audit security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: No. It reviews skill structure, metadata, documentation quality, and decomposition signals.

faq.question: Can it audit multiple skills at once?
faq.answer: Yes. The bundled script can audit a local directory containing multiple skill folders.

faq.question: Does it change files automatically?
faq.answer: The bundled script shown here audits and reports. Manual review is still needed before changes.

faq.question: What files does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects each skill folder to contain a SKILL.md file with YAML frontmatter.

faq.question: When should a skill be split?
faq.answer: Split a skill when it is too long, has many concerns, or contains complex workflows.

faq.question: Is it suitable for marketplace reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides consistent quality checks, but marketplace reviewers should add security and policy review.
